quote_of_day.text:
>>29865
Am from the lineage of landlords, the true uppercastes. It's us people who keep chamars in check, without us you pongas will become sissies of chamars. We are your jajmaan, pyaare ponge know your place.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of longing settled over the board as one anon lamented a lost love from 10th class. The post drew a gentle sprinkle of responses.

A high-pressure front of caste debate developed, sparked by the relaunch of SGTOW and further fueled by discussions around Bollywood stars' caste and faith. The debate brought scattered squalls of argument.

Meanwhile, a brief but intense downpour of insults and claims occurred as an anon declared 'raandput' status and another questioned the sexual behavior of Cucktri women.

These showers of controversy passed through quickly, leaving behind an overcast of tension.

articles.title: Anon regrets lost love from 10th class
articles.body:
Anon posted about a past relationship with a girlfriend from 10th class, claiming she 'jainwinly loved' him but he 'messed it up' and now misses her.

He described her as 'very petite, nice looking' and recalled helping her prepare for boards while she taught him Urdu. Anon's post drew various responses from other users. One anon shared their own struggles with freelancing and delusions of being an army officer.

Another anon described imagining scenarios where they yell at people, only to end up yelling at nothing. Some users responded with criticism.

One asked if Anon had worked on himself or still acted like a 'street shitting LC subhuman.' Another suggested arrange marriage as an option and mocked those who fantasize about 'negro cocks.' Anon provided more details about his past relationship, revealing he was 'very hot tempered' and yelled at his girlfriend over small stuff.

He also mentioned she initially contacted him on 'bhungigram' after seeing him at a neighbor's birthday party. Other users made judgments about Anon's appearance and behavior.

One stated Anon likely looks 'subhuman.' Another asked how Anon had 'messed up' with his girlfriend, sparking a discussion about Anon's past actions. Anon's current situation and interests were also shared.

He mentioned pursuing an MSc through distance learning and having a freelancing contract that pays between 20,000 to 24,000 per month.

articles.title: SGTOW Relaunch Sparks Caste Debate
articles.body:
Anon announced the restart of SGTOW, stating they are taking up the mantle.

One respondent referenced old SGTOW threads, mentioning that eating meat is one of the few scriptural privileges Hindu shastras give to shudras, and that Vaishnavism is a dwija-coded religion. They argued shudras should not be Vaishnava.

Another anon countered that jatis have always been autonomous entities and that they couldn't care less which way others go, unless it's another movement like lundbedkarism.

A different poster claimed Vaishnavas were pro-shudra and mentioned Kashmir shaivas are more strict vegetarians. They suggested that without Vaishnavism, many lower castes would have converted.

However, a respondent made an anti-Semitic remark, saying they are Jewish and that jeets are born to be their slaves. They also made a threat to fund radical Muslim groups.

Other comments included discussions about caste dynamics, with one saying shudras should give their daughters to higher castes. A respondent joked about Bhangali "UC" and another made a remark about shudra girls serving rajput landlords.

The conversation also touched on vegetarianism and veganism, with one anon saying everyone in the thread must follow Vaishnava veganism with no caste discrimination.

articles.title: Anon debates caste, faith of Bollywood stars
articles.body:
Anon started a thread discussing the Bollywood film Kabir Singh, listing several points about its plot and characters.

One respondent described the movie as bad and claimed it excused hot-tempered men, while another Anon questioned the religion and caste of the film's stars, Kabir and Preity.

Anon noted that Khatris are often Hindu, and Preity, a Sikh, was in an interfaith marriage with Kabir, a Hindu Khatri. Another Anon identified Preity as a Jatt Sikh and Kabir as likely a Khatri Hindu.

The conversation turned to the topic of caste, with Anon stating that only South Indian Brahmins are considered Upper Caste, while another Anon argued that Jatts and Baniyas are not Lower Caste.

A respondent called out another Anon for baiting and making inflammatory statements about castes.

Anon also discussed the film's casting and the real-life castes of actors, with some speculating about the stars' backgrounds and others making jokes about their appearances.

articles.title: Anon claims 'raandput' status, sparks debate
articles.body:
Anon posted a series of messages invoking 'raandput' status, listing 'rota reh raandput', 'rota reh ponge', 'rota reh chamar', and others.

Another anon responded, questioning the legitimacy of some groups claiming 'rajput' status, citing that some castes like 'cucktri' and 'goojars' were not kangs.

A respondent argued that those who know their clan, gotra, kuldevi, and thikana can identify real rajputs, while fake surname users often fail to provide such details.

Anon also joked about the current state of caste identity, stating 'feudalism is owar yaar, there are kangs anymore'. One poster sarcastically noted that even auto drivers can claim to be rajputs these days.

articles.title: Anon Questions Cucktri Women's Sexual Behavior
articles.body:
Anon posted about Cucktri women being "cock hungry" and mentioned Sunny Leone, a well-known Indian adult film star from a Bohra Cucktri family. They claimed that Cucktri women tend to suck "mad cocks" and don't value religion.

Another anon replied, asking what makes Cucktri women so "cock hungry" and if they marry the men they fuck outside of their caste.

One respondent suggested that Cucktri women are attractive, with fair complexion and symmetrical faces, which might contribute to their behavior.

Anon also discussed the history of Cucktri and other castes, mentioning that Gujjaris and Jatnis formed a majority of concubines during the Rajput and Mughal eras. The conversation included various claims and jokes about Cucktri women's sexual behavior and attractiveness.
